world.title: Iran war: US peace talks near end, naval blockade tightens
world.description: US President Trump indicates Iran war is close to ending with potential peace talks, while the US enforces a naval blockade on Iranian ports. Read more at straitstimes.com.

world.title: Sligo: Very rare Greenland shark discovered washed up on beach
world.description: The Greenland shark, which can have a lifespan of over 500 years, was discovered in Sligo on Saturday.

science.description: When Artemis II astronaut Christina Koch returned from a historic journey in space, her dog Sadie welcomed her at the doorstep. This reunion is what the internet cannot stop talking about.

science.title: Cosmic dust found to be behind Venusian haze
science.description: Cosmic dust from shooting stars, not Venus itself, is responsible for a puzzling haze layer beneath the planet’s sulfuric acid clouds, new research reveals. At AQN, we like the odd, distracting flight of fancy into hitherto uncharted air quality realms, so imagine our joy when, having published a story on…

science.title: NASA's Latest Rover Images Reveal Possible Winged Insect and Predator Life Forms on Mars
science.description: Ohio University entomologist examines NASA Curiosity rover images and claims they reveal insect and reptile forms on the Martian surface.

science.title: Scientists now know why we saw so many fireballs in March. 'A dying...'
science.description: Meteors rained down on Earth in April, and the source remained unclear. A study has now found a cluster of meteors which is possibly the culprit. There is a debris field and our planet is passing through it at the moment. We have a new meteor shower as well.

science.title: Artemis 3 and beyond: To set foot on the Moon, NASA needs Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos, but their spacecraft aren’t ready
science.description: The US space agency is already assembling an SLS rocket and an Orion capsule to launch the next mission in 2027. The lunar landers from SpaceX and Blue Origin are prototypes that are years behind schedule

science.title: DESI Completes Planned 3D Map of the Universe and Continues Exploring
science.description: The Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument has complete its originally planned 5-year mission and mapped more than 47 million galaxies and quasars, creating the largest high-resolution 3D map of our universe to date. Because of the instrument's excellent performance and hints that dark energy might evolve, DESI will continue observations into 2028 and further expand the map.

science.title: The Starship V3 static fire everyone was waiting for just happened
science.description: SpaceX completed a full 33-engine static fire of Starship V3 Booster 19 today at Pad 2, clearing the final ground test milestone before Flight 12 — the most powerful rocket debut in history.

ai.title: India's Data Centre Boom: A Power Surge in Digital Infrastructure
ai.description: India's data centre sector is set for massive growth, with capacity expected to reach up to 5 GW by 2030. Driven by AI adoption and digital transformation, the market is anticipated to garner $60-70 billion in investments over the next five years, positioning the country as a digital hub in Asia-Pacific.

ai.title: Snap to Lay Off 1,000 Employees, Cut Costs by $500M Amid AI Push
ai.description: Snap Inc. will lay off about 1,000 employees, or 16% of its workforce, as it looks to cut costs by $500M and leverage AI to boost productivity. The move follows pressure from activist investor Irenic Capital and comes amid wider tech industry job cuts.

ai.title: Infosys Partners with Tennis Prodigy Carlos Alcaraz: A New Era of Innovation
ai.description: Infosys has announced a long-term partnership with Carlos Alcaraz, the youngest athlete to achieve World No. 1 in men's tennis. As Infosys' Global Brand Ambassador, Alcaraz will collaborate on AI-powered match analytics and tech-for-good initiatives. The alliance celebrates a blend of innovation and athletic excellence.
